channel.title: Starmer Watch — Pressure Index
channel.description: Composite pressure index, top news, and market signals for Keir Starmer's leadership.

item.title: Pressure Index 56/100 (elevated)
item.description: Public pressure bloc is above the modeled 81 MP trigger. Pressure Index 56/100 (elevated). Polymarket's top Starmer-exit signal is 17.5%.

item.title: Guardian (Open Platform): The case for Labour to introduce a wealth tax has never been stronger | Phillip Inman
item.description: The case for Labour to introduce a wealth tax has never been stronger | Phillip Inman

item.title:
Bluesky · @georgeeaton.bsky.social: Getting more people into work is a long-term project – it won’t suddenly fund higher defence spending (as Burnham suggests).
Real choices are: raise taxes on most people/raise borrowing/cut spending.
item.description:
Getting more people into work is a long-term project – it won’t suddenly fund higher defence spending (as Burnham suggests).
Real choices are: raise taxes on most people/raise borrowing/cut spending.

item.title: Independent UK Politics: Restore Britain set to sweep Burnham to victory in Makerfield, new poll suggests
item.description: Restore Britain set to sweep Burnham to victory in Makerfield, new poll suggests

item.title: Independent UK Politics: Burnham warns UK is heading for ‘poisonous’ politics of US under Starmer leadership
item.description: Burnham warns UK is heading for ‘poisonous’ politics of US under Starmer leadership

item.title: LabourList: New ministers in Defence, Home Office, Defra and Whips Office
item.description: New ministers in Defence, Home Office, Defra and Whips Office

item.title: Bluesky · @georgeeaton.bsky.social: Really interesting back and forth in the comments here. I think what an engaged prime minister would have actually done (in *February 2025* when HMT-MOD deadlock happened!) is increase funding and put in a Secretary o...
item.description: Really interesting back and forth in the comments here. I think what an engaged prime minister would have actually done (in *February 2025* when HMT-MOD deadlock happened!) is increase funding and put in a Secretary o...

item.title: BBC Politics: Chris Mason: Starmer defiant after defence spending row
item.description: Chris Mason: Starmer defiant after defence spending row

item.title: Guardian (Open Platform): Public control of water and energy at heart of Burnham agenda, sources say
